answer:
I think being overly popular can actually have some downsides. You know, when everyone knows you, there's this constant pressure to maintain that image, and it can be exhausting. Sometimes, people might judge you based on assumptions rather than who you really are. I mean, take social media, for example. It can create this illusion of happiness and success, but behind the scenes, it might lead to anxiety or loneliness because you feel like you always have to be on display.

As for why people crave fame, it seems to stem from a desire for validation and recognition. Society often glorifies celebrities, and many believe that being famous equates to being happy or fulfilled. It's appealing because fame can bring opportunities, like traveling or experiencing unique events. But, at the same time, that spotlight can be blinding—it strips away privacy and normalcy.

When it comes to deciding whether it's better to be nice or popular, I personally lean towards being nice. You know, genuine connections and friendships matter more than a large following. A few close friends who appreciate me for who I am feels way more rewarding than superficial interactions with a crowd of admirers. Plus, kindness tends to attract positivity, which leads to more genuine relationships in the long run. So, while popularity has its perks, I find that being nice and authentic is ultimately more fulfilling.
title: What's bad about being too popular? Why do people want to be famous? Which is better - being nice or being popular? Why?
